taxonomy: Senecio papuanus belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its composite flowers and leaf arrangement. Within the family Asteraceae, Senecio papuanus is classified in the genus Senecio, which includes a large number of species commonly known as ragworts or groundels. intro: Senecio papuanus, also known as Papuan ragwort, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the island of New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Senecio papuanus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Senecio papuanus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues.
